channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Trump official-turned-foe John Bolton accepts plea deal in documents case
item.description: Despite serving as Trump's national security adviser, Bolton has emerged as a top critic of the president.

item.title: Israel must allow ICRC to visit Palestinians in prison, Supreme Court rules
item.description: Israel’s Supreme Court rejects government ban on prisoner visits, affirming Red Cross access under international law.

item.title: Ronaldo, Messi at World Cup: Who else is playing their final tournament?
item.description: The World Cup is set to be the swansong for some big names, including Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi, but who else?

item.title: Russia’s Andreeva beats Ukraine’s Kostyuk in no-handshake French Open semi
item.description: No handshake and no photo as Mirra Andreeva of Russia beats Marta Kostyuk of Ukraine to reach French Open final.

item.title: The making of Sudan’s RSF
item.description: Sudan’s Rapid Support Forces have a long history of violations, going back to when they were known as the ‘Janjaweed'.

item.title: Pro-Palestinian badges should not be worn by NHS staff, UK review suggests
item.description: Anti-Semitism review recommends that badges proclaiming political support should not be worn by public health workers

item.title: ‘Unpatriotic’: Trump decries Republicans who voted to constrain Iran war
item.description: Trump takes aim at lawmakers after House passes war powers resolution; advocates call on Senate to advance the measure.

item.title: MAHA moms test their influence in US glyphosate fight
item.description: 'Make America Healthy Again' activists see legislative wins, White House disappointments and questions for the midterms.

item.title: Black Lung Rising
item.description: Fault Lines investigates a surge in black lung among younger coal miners in Appalachia, and gaps in dust protections.

item.title: Lebanon’s latest truce: What is different from the April agreement?
item.description: The ceasefire's prospects appear uncertain before its implementation has even started.

item.title: Thousands protest in Albania against coastal resort linked to Jared Kushner
item.description: Environmental organisations have called for the project's suspension, citing threats to biodiversity.

item.title: Iran footballers describe how war with US-Israel affects their World Cup
item.description: Iran national players Saeid Ezatolahi and Mohammad Ghorbani talk about how war is overshadowing a World Cup dream.

item.title: Shark predicts winning start for Brazil at FIFA World Cup
item.description: Shark predicts winning start for Brazil at FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Why is a US Ebola facility in Kenya sparking protests?
item.description: The plan has drawn backlash from Kenyans as well as the country's union of healthcare workers.

item.title: Fearful foreign nationals in South Africa forced out of their homes
item.description: Anti-immigration violence in South Africa has forced foreign migrants to flee their homes.

item.title: Rights group sues South African government over US arms exports
item.description: Case challenges permits approved by South Africa’s arms control body, citing compliance failures and oversight gaps.

item.title: Archaeologists find ancient matrilineal society in Turkiye’s Catalhoyuk
item.description: About 9,000 years ago, the settlement was an egalitarian, matrilineal society with no evidence of organised violence.

item.title: Fighting in Somalia’s capital as anger over election delay erupts
item.description: Residents flee from their homes due to fighting between two warring political factions.

item.title: First US screwworm case in 60 years: Should America be worried?
item.description: The US Department of Agriculture says New World screwworm has been detected in a Texas calf.

item.title: What do Turkiye and Indonesia want from bilateral talks?
item.description: Turkiye's Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan travelled to Indonesia to cement the two states' growing partnership.

item.title: N Korea’s Kim Jong Un calls for ‘exponential’ expansion of nuclear arsenal
item.description: Kim says expansion necessary due to worsening security threats, long-term confrontations with 'most ferocious enemies'.

item.title: Who are the World Cup Golden Boot favourites? Mbappe, Ronaldo, Messi, Kane?
item.description: There are a host of top players who could claim the coveted title of top goal scorer, including two from the same team.

item.title: Israel’s Smotrich announces plan for 2,162 homes in occupied West Bank
item.description: The far-right finance minister says the new houses will 'strengthen our hold on the land'.

item.title: Man City threatens legal action against Real Madrid following Haaland claim
item.description: Enrique Riquelme held up a Real Madrid shirt bearing Erling Haaland's name, while campaigning for club presidency.

item.title: Quiz: World Cup 2026 – player and team records, key statistics
item.description: Test your football knowledge before the World Cup begins with a 10-question quiz.
